research_confidence.notes: Architecture and content types well-documented via KA engineering blog and open-source repos (Perseus, Wonder Blocks). Exact accessibility tree roles (AXButton vs AXLink) for specific buttons are inferred from standard React/WCAG patterns since direct macOS accessibility tree dumps were not available. Button text ('Check', 'Skip', 'Next question') confirmed from KA support articles and community posts. Timing values are conservative estimates based on SPA behavior with GraphQL backend and Fastly CDN. Perseus widget types confirmed from GitHub source (30+ widget dirs). Khanmigo AI tutor may introduce unexpected modals on paid/district accounts.

timing.notes: React SPA with GraphQL Federation backend (Go services + Python monolith) on Google Cloud via Fastly CDN. Page transitions are client-side route changes (no full reload) but involve GraphQL data fetching. Exercise question transitions within a set are faster (~1.5s) since they swap content in-place via Perseus. Video pages load YouTube iframe which adds latency. Initial page loads slower than in-page transitions. Wonder Blocks design system components used throughout. Conservative estimates account for slow network and dynamic content hydration.

never_click.reason: Permanently deletes user account and all progress data
Ends the session; re-authentication required to continue
Alternative text for logout; ends session
Teacher-facing action that removes a student from a class
Removes the learner from an enrolled class
Opens a report dialog for content issues; does not advance learning and may flag account for unusual activity. Contains hidden 'Show Answer' option.
Navigates away from Khan Academy to YouTube; video progress will NOT be tracked and no credit is awarded
Opens donation page; not related to coursework
Opens account settings; not related to coursework and could change configuration

Course-wide navigation arrow (~23x25px AXButton at top of page). Skips past the entire current unit. Never the right target for lesson-by-lesson progression. Use 'Up next: <title>' AXLink for post-video advancement; use sidebar lesson links for within-unit nav.
Course-wide navigation arrow that skips backward past completed content. Never the right target for forward progression.

platform_quirks.description: Khanmigo AI tutor (GPT-4 based) may appear as a floating button or modal dialog on exercise pages for paid/district accounts ($9/month or district-provided). It presents as a chat overlay that can obscure exercise content. If detected, it should be dismissed (close button, typically an X in top-right of overlay) before interacting with the exercise. The Khanmigo icon is usually in the bottom-right corner.

After completing a practice exercise with exactly 1 incorrect answer, a 'Bonus question' button may appear. If offered, the bonus question MUST be either answered or skipped — leaving it unanswered means the exercise is NOT marked complete and no credit is given. Answering correctly upgrades score to Proficient. Some exercises (reading comprehension, teacher-locked, small question banks) do not offer bonus questions.

The 'Skip' button on exercises automatically marks the question as INCORRECT. It also shows a step-by-step solution/hint walkthrough. The old separate 'Hint' button has been replaced by this Skip flow. Do NOT click Skip unless the system is stuck or unable to answer. The Skip button grays out after a question is successfully answered.

Videos must be watched in their entirety (every second) to be marked as complete. Skipping even a few seconds results in an incomplete status. Playback speed up to 2x is allowed and still counts for completion. For teacher-assigned videos, 90% must be watched at no greater than 2x speed. The video must play to the very end — closing early even with seconds remaining marks incomplete.

Under heavy traffic or network restrictions (e.g., YouTube blocked), Khan Academy may serve a fallback video player instead of the YouTube embed. This player lacks speed controls and subtitle customization. A 'Switch to full player' link appears at bottom-right to return to YouTube player.

Mastery Challenges (math courses only) have a 12-hour window to complete once started, and only one can be taken per 12-hour period. They consist of exactly 6 questions covering 3 skills (2 questions each). They appear on the main Course page only when specific conditions are met (Familiar on 3+ skills, Proficient on 1+ skill). Questions are randomized so 2 questions per skill may not be consecutive.

Math expression inputs require specific formatting. Parentheses must be used explicitly (e.g., '1/(2p)' not '1/2p'). Square brackets shown in hints should be entered as parentheses. The expression evaluator follows strict PEMDAS order of operations. Formatting errors cause correct answers to be marked wrong.

First-time or returning users may see a 'Welcome to Khan Academy!' modal asking 'So we can give you the right tools, let us know if you're a... Learner / Teacher / Parent'. This must be dismissed before content is accessible. District admin goal-setting popups may also appear on login. Cookie consent banner may appear on first visit.

The left sidebar navigation panel opens by default but can be collapsed by clicking a left-pointing arrow button. If collapsed, lesson activity items are not visible in the accessibility tree for navigation. The sidebar must be expanded (click right-pointing arrow) to navigate between activities within a lesson.

Quizzes, Unit Tests, and Course Challenges are sequential — once you advance past a question, you cannot go back to it. The 'Skip' button marks it as incorrect permanently for that attempt. Course Challenges contain 30 questions. Unit Tests cover all unit skills. Mastery Challenges have 6 questions.

Khan Academy exercises use the Perseus system with 30+ widget types including: radio (multiple choice), input-number, numeric-input, expression (math), interactive-graphs (Mafs library, rebuilt for a11y), categorizer, sorter, orderer, dropdown, matcher, graded-group, graded-group-set, label-image, table, passage, plotter, free-response, matrix, number-line, definition, explanation, image, measurer, and more. Each widget type has different interaction patterns. Some (interactive-graph, sorter, orderer, matcher) require drag-and-drop or complex mouse interactions that may need screenshot-based LLM guidance.

On unit/course navigation pages, clicking video or article items may open content in a modal overlay rather than navigating to a new page. The modal has the video's title as a hyperlink at the top — clicking the title navigates to the full lesson page. This modal behavior is inconsistent and may differ between content types.

UNIVERSAL RULE: Before calling send_to_llm to choose an answer, ALL hidden content must be extracted into the prompt context. For dropdowns/comboboxes, this means: open EACH popup, enumerate its menu items via discover_menu (role=AXMenuItem), capture the option list, close the popup, then call send_to_llm with the full options on every popup. The LLM must NEVER be asked to guess options from screenshot+context alone — it gets the literal AX-rendered option strings so its returned answer matches what the click handler will see. Same principle applies to any hidden/collapsed UI: modals, expandable sections, tooltips with answer content, etc.

completion_model.video: Must watch every second of the video (no skipping). Playback up to 2x is fine. Completion shown as full colored bar with checkmark icon in sidebar. For assignments: 90% watched at ≤2x speed.
completion_model.article: Auto-completed when visited and scrolled through. No explicit 'Mark as complete' button. Simply opening and scrolling to the bottom registers as read. Green checkmark appears in sidebar.
completion_model.exercise: Must answer all questions in the set (typically 4-7 questions). >70% correct = 'Practiced' level. All correct = 'Proficient'. With bonus question: 1 wrong + bonus correct = 'Proficient'. Exercise NOT complete until all questions (including bonus if offered) are answered or skipped.
completion_model.quiz: Must answer all questions. Scored as percentage. Results affect mastery levels for skills covered. No going back to previous questions.
completion_model.unit_test: Must answer all questions. Results can level skills up or down. Covers all skills in the unit. No going back.
completion_model.course_challenge: 30 questions covering entire course. Results affect mastery levels across all course skills. No going back.
completion_model.mastery_challenge: 6 questions covering 3 skills (2 each). Math courses only. 12-hour window. 2 correct on a skill = level up, 2 wrong = level down, 1 each = no change.

bot_detection.known_measures: Khan Academy uses Fastly CDN which provides basic bot detection and rate limiting. No CAPTCHA documented for normal browsing. The platform tracks video watching behavior closely (scrub detection, play state monitoring via YouTube API) to prevent credit gaming. Google Cloud App Engine has default DDoS protections. Static assets served from kastatic.org and kasandbox.org domains. No explicit Cloudflare or dedicated bot management layer documented in public sources.
bot_detection.recommended_delays: Add 1-2s random delay between actions. For exercises, wait at least 2s after Check before clicking Next. For video pages, do not interact with the player programmatically faster than a human would. Avoid submitting answers faster than 5s per question to prevent pattern detection. Between page navigations, wait 2-3s minimum. For rapid sequential exercises, vary timing between 3-8s per question.

EXERCISE.description: Exercise screens present a question (rendered by the Perseus system) with various interactive widgets for answering. The question appears in the main content area. At the bottom-right are 'Check' and 'Skip' buttons. A progress indicator (dots or fraction like '3 of 7') shows position in the question set. After checking, correct answers show green with a ding sound; incorrect answers show red. Left sidebar shows current position within the lesson. Exercise types include practice (4-7 questions), quizzes, unit tests, course challenges (30 questions), and mastery challenges (6 questions).

subtypes.name: multiple_choice
subtypes.description: Radio button selection from 4-5 answer choices. Most common exercise type (~74% of exercises produce number or MC response). Each choice is a clickable radio button with text/math label.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click radio button for desired answer -> click 'Check'

subtypes.name: multiple_select
subtypes.description: Checkbox selection where multiple answers may be correct. Instructions say 'Select all that apply' or 'Choose all answers that apply'.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click one or more checkboxes -> click 'Check'

subtypes.name: numeric_input
subtypes.description: Text field expecting a numeric answer. May have math keypad overlay. Field accepts typed numbers. Perseus widgets: input-number, numeric-input.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click text field -> type numeric answer -> click 'Check'

subtypes.name: expression_input
subtypes.description: Math expression field accepting algebraic expressions, fractions, exponents. Uses specialized math input with LaTeX rendering. Requires precise formatting (parentheses matter, PEMDAS enforced). Perseus widget: expression.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click expression field -> type math expression (use parentheses carefully) -> click 'Check'

subtypes.name: dropdown
subtypes.description: One or more dropdown menus embedded in a sentence or passage. User selects the correct option from each dropdown. Perseus widget: dropdown.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click dropdown -> select option from popup list -> repeat for all dropdowns -> click 'Check'

operational_notes.screen_variant: ANY inline dropdown(s)/combobox(es) on Khan Perseus exercises
operational_notes.rule: ENUMERATE OPTIONS BEFORE ASK. Verified BT engine primitives only.
operational_notes.note: Khan AXComboBox dropdowns have placeholder name='Select an answer' until clicked, and options are NOT in the AX tree until the popup opens. Universal pattern: open each popup, enumerate options via discover_menu, close, store options on the popup item via store_to_current; THEN call send_to_llm with all popups (each carrying real options); THEN iterate to select. Lookup keys by $popup.description (NOT $popup.label — placeholder collides across popups). find_and_click accepts element=$ref from a for_each-bound popup, no separate selector needed.
verified_bt_engine_primitives: for_each items=$list variable=popup do={...} — binds current item as $popup
store: 'key' on action — writes return to blackboard[key]
store_to_current: 'field' inside for_each — writes return to current popup dict
find_and_click with element=$popup.element — accepts pre-resolved element ref
discover_menu role=AXMenuItem returns list of option strings
select_dropdown_option owns its own open/strategy ladder/normalize/verify

params.question: Fill in each dropdown with the correct option based on the screenshot and the surrounding question context. Each item's `label` is its position; each item's `options` lists the actual menu choices available for THAT dropdown. Return the EXACT option string from options[] for each item.

known_failure_modes: Element staleness: if Wonder Blocks re-renders popups after one opens, $popup.element from outer find_all may go stale on iter 2+. If that happens (only first dropdown opens, rest fail with element-not-found), the workaround would be sequential unrolled blocks (find_all + index access) per popup — but try the canonical for_each version first.

ccm_observed_failures.16:54_observation: find_and_click element=$popup.element → resolves target='' role=None → ACTION RETURNED NONE
ccm_observed_failures.root_cause: find_and_click handler does NOT read 'element' param. Only 'click'/'click_element' do.
ccm_observed_failures.fix: Use 'click' action with element=$popup (whole dict, not just .element)
ccm_observed_failures.17:00_observation: click element=$popup → re-find by description='Select an answer' (placeholder) fails because handle_click hardcodes role='AXPopUpButton', but Khan uses AXComboBox. Re-find returns None.
ccm_observed_failures.17:00_fix: Pass element=$popup.element (raw AX ref, not dict) — handler skips re-find entirely and uses the ref directly.
ccm_observed_failures.17:10_observation: All 3 popups successfully enumerated (click + discover_menu + escape worked end-to-end). send_to_llm crashed: claude_cli exit 1 with empty stderr, /api/v1/generate returned 500. API log showed question='' — likely Claude CLI needs a non-empty question.
ccm_observed_failures.17:10_fix: Add 'question' field to send_to_llm.params in the canonical BT template.
ccm_observed_failures.17:15_observation: Enumeration phase fully working (all 3 popups opened/enumerated/closed). send_to_llm returned matches but with only 2 unique keys for 3 popups (matches_keys=['Select an answer', 'The forces act on'] — both colliding). Lookup_match for popup_1 picked the value keyed under 'The forces act on' which was actually the answer for a DIFFERENT popup, so click on this popup's menu (options=['the same not selected', 'different']) failed to find that text.
ccm_observed_failures.17:15_fix:
1. parse_matching_response now also keys matches[str(idx)] = best.
2. Worker generates UNROLLED selection phase with literal '0','1','2' keys.
ccm_observed_failures.17:22_observation: Enumeration phase ran, but item[0].options=[] in api log — popup 0's discover_menu found 0 AXMenuItem because menu hadn't rendered yet (0.7s post_delay too short on first popup open). LLM got empty options for item 1, returned empty answer, matches['0']='', lookup_match returned empty, select_dropdown_option failed with 'missing option' param.
ccm_observed_failures.17:22_fix: Bump click post_delay 0.7→1.2s + insert wait_for_element AXMenuItem timeout=2.0s between click and discover_menu so discover_menu only fires once a menu is actually rendered.
ccm_observed_failures.17:25_observation: Enumeration phase fully worked (all 3 popups, wait_for_element added). discover_menu returned options like ['the same not', 'different'] — the 'the same' option's AX name 'the same not selected' got 9 chars stripped by Mac's _extract_menu_items (which strips ' selected'), leaving 'the same not'. LLM picked 'the same not' (the only thing on offer). select_dropdown_option opened the popup and normalized menu items to ['the same', 'different'] — 'the same not' was no match → click failed.
ccm_observed_failures.17:25_fix: Server-side workaround in _solve_matching_with_gemini: strip dangling ' not' suffix from options before passing to LLM (undoes Mac's overzealous strip). Real fix would be Mac-side: _extract_menu_items should strip ' not selected' (13 chars) before falling back to ' selected' (9 chars). Mac is frozen, so server workaround is the pragmatic path.

subtypes.name: interactive_graph
subtypes.description: Graphing widget where users plot points, draw lines, or manipulate graph elements. Built with Mafs React library. Supports keyboard and screen reader. Perseus widget: interactive-graphs. Includes number-line widget.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: interact with graph (drag points, draw lines, use keyboard arrows) -> click 'Check'. Complex — likely needs screenshot + LLM visual analysis.

subtypes.name: sorter_orderer
subtypes.description: Items that must be arranged in correct order (orderer) or sorted into categories (sorter/categorizer). Drag-and-drop interface. Perseus widgets: sorter, orderer, categorizer.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: drag items to correct positions or categories -> click 'Check'. May need screenshot + LLM analysis for correct ordering.

operational_notes.rule: DRAG BBOX FROM TREE.JSON + correct nested-dict schema
operational_notes.note: Khan Perseus ranking/sorter widgets present draggable handle elements in the AX tree. find_all does NOT surface visible_bbox in its output, but the full tree.json saved to the consult dir DOES contain visible_bbox: [x, y, width, height] as a 4-element list on every node with positional data (~731 such entries on a typical Khan exercise tree). The worker generating this BT has tree.json available — read drag handle bboxes directly from there, then emit drag actions with correct schema.
operational_notes.drag_handler_schema: drag.params MUST be: {start: {x: <int>, y: <int>}, end: {x: <int>, y: <int>}}. Nested dicts, NOT flat source_x/source_y/target_x/target_y or start_x/start_y. Handler reads start['x'] / start['y'] / end['x'] / end['y'] — anything else returns 'missing/invalid start/end coords' and BT fails.
operational_notes.bt_construction_pattern: 1) Identify draggable handle elements in tree.json (typically AXButton or AXGenericElement nodes containing the rankable items — look at their name/description for the item labels like 'G', 'B', 'R' or full names). 2) Read each handle's visible_bbox = [x, y, w, h]. Center = (x + w/2, y + h/2). 3) send_to_llm with screenshot + question_type=solve_complex; ask for the correct ORDERING (e.g. 'R,B,G' for greatest-to-least). 4) For each item that needs to move: compute source_center (current position) and target_center (the position where it SHOULD be — typically the bbox center of where the target-position handle currently is). 5) Emit one drag action per move with correct nested schema.

params.question: <read screenshot + tree.json bboxes; return ordering as comma-separated letters>

anti_patterns: drag with FLAT keys source_x/source_y/target_x/target_y or start_x/start_y/end_x/end_y — handler rejects, BT fails
Skipping the drag step and only sending to LLM + clicking Check — answer stays in default order, Khan marks wrong
Using fixed coords without consulting tree.json bboxes — coords shift per session/viewport

subtypes.name: ranking
subtypes.description: Perseus sorter widget operating as a RANKING list (single column, 3+ items to be arranged top-to-bottom by some criterion: mass, size, magnitude, etc.). Distinct from sorter_orderer's category-bucket use case. Variant name suffix: RANKING_DRAG.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: evaluate items by criterion (via screenshot) -> drag handles to reorder if needed -> ALWAYS perform at least one drag to engage widget (Khan disables Check until widget is touched) -> click 'Check'.

operational_notes.research_source: Perplexity Deep Research (live Perseus repo source). Saved verbatim: /home/user/taey-ed/consultations/REVIEWS/perseus_sorter_widget_dr_20260518.md. Confidence on central claim: HIGH.
operational_notes.screen_variant: EXERCISE_RANKING_DRAG (Khan Perseus sorter widget, vertical single-column ranking)
operational_notes.PROTOCOL_one_shot: You are the BT compiler. You have BOTH the screenshot AND tree.json. Synthesize the drag plan AT GENERATION TIME and bake concrete drag actions into the BT. DO NOT defer to runtime. DO NOT punt to UNKNOWN. The handler set is sufficient — drag + find_and_click is all you need.
operational_notes.perseus_internals_OBSERVED: Check button is gated by a single React state boolean: userInput.changed. validateSorter() returns {type: invalid} while !userInput.changed. The flag is set ONLY by Sorter.handleChange, which is wired to Sortable's onChange prop. onChange fires from: (a) onMouseUp(key) after drag-release, (b) moveOptionToIndex programmatic API. NOT from onMouseMove, NOT from HTML5 dragstart/dragover/drop (widget uses jQuery+React mouse events, no HTML5 drag API at all).
operational_notes.tickle_drag_VERIFIED: A zero-displacement drag (mousedown → mouseup at same coords) DOES set changed:true. Mechanism: onChange fires from onMouseUp regardless of whether items[] reordered. Sortable.onMouseMove only reorders items[] when newIndex != currentIndex, but that does NOT gate onChange. Confirmed by Perplexity Deep Research direct from Perseus source.
operational_notes.macos_cgevent_sequence_REQUIRED: kCGEventLeftMouseDown @ item center → (delay ≥1 rAF ~16ms) → kCGEventLeftMouseDragged @ same-or-displaced position → kCGEventLeftMouseUp @ target position. Our Mac drag handler (mousedown → 80ms hold → 18 dragged moves @ 20ms → 50ms hold → mouseup) satisfies this regardless of start==end since it always emits intermediate dragged events. Use the existing `drag` handler as-is.
BT_GENERATION_RULES: Step 1: Find the AXList containing the sortable items in tree.json (look for AXList with ~3-5 AXGroup children at consistent x-coord, small width ~30-40px — grip column).
Step 2: For each AXGroup child of that AXList, compute center = (x + w/2, y + h/2) from its visible_bbox. Call these POSITIONS p[0]=top, p[1]=mid, p[2]=bottom, ... by y-order.
Step 3: Read the screenshot. Identify the visible label at each position (e.g., p[0]='G', p[1]='B', p[2]='R'). Call this CURRENT.
Step 4: Read the question. Determine CORRECT order top-to-bottom by the question's criterion (e.g., greatest-to-least mass).
Step 5a: If CURRENT == CORRECT: emit ONE tickle drag at p[1] (mid item) — start and end at same coords. This engages the widget (changed:true) without reordering.

Step 5b: If CURRENT != CORRECT: emit sequential bubble-sort drags. For each position i in 0..N-1, if current[i] != correct[i], find correct[i] in current at index j, emit drag {start: p[j], end: p[i]}, then mentally swap current[i] and current[j]. Repeat until current == correct.
Step 6: Append wait_for_element Check max_wait=3.0, then find_and_click Check role=AXButton strategy=mouse_click match_mode=exact post_delay=2.5.
All drag coords MUST come from tree.json bboxes — NEVER hardcode from prior consults; the viewport may have shifted.
operational_notes.drag_handler_schema_REMINDER: drag.params MUST be nested dicts: {start: {x: <int>, y: <int>}, end: {x: <int>, y: <int>}, post_delay: <float>}. NOT flat source_x/source_y/start_x/start_y/target_x/target_y. Handler rejects flat schema and BT fails.
anti_patterns: Returning screen_type='UNKNOWN' with a wait BT — this is the punt-to-runtime failure mode. The handler set IS sufficient. YOU are the compiler. Synthesize the drags now.

Calling send_to_llm to ask Pro for an answer AT RUNTIME — unnecessary; you have the screenshot already at BT-generation time. Decide the order yourself, bake concrete drag coords into the BT, optionally include a store_qa with the answer for course-knowledge persistence.
Skipping ALL drags and clicking Check immediately — Check stays disabled because userInput.changed is false.
Drag with flat keys (source_x/source_y/start_x/start_y/target_x/target_y) — handler rejects.
Hardcoding coords from a prior consult — viewport shifts; always re-read tree.json bboxes.
Clicking 'Make image bigger' or 'Skip' — wrong targets.

subtypes.name: matcher
subtypes.description: Match items from two columns. Connect related items. Perseus widget: matcher.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: match items between columns (drag or select) -> click 'Check'. May need screenshot analysis.

subtypes.name: label_image
subtypes.description: Image with hotspots or blank labels to fill in. User clicks dots/regions and selects or types labels. Perseus widget: label-image.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click labeled points on image -> select/type answer for each -> click 'Check'

operational_notes.note: Wonder Blocks renders dropdowns as AXComboBox at depth ~32. Each combobox's value attribute carries the dot description (e.g. 'a sphere with a negative sign located outside the center of the atom'). find_and_click can match these by value substring + role=AXComboBox + match_mode=contains. After click, the dropdown options render as AXMenuItem at SHALLOW depth (~14, OUTSIDE the AXWebArea). handle_find_and_click for AXMenuItem first searches web-area scope (returns nothing for these popups), then falls through to find_element which walks full app and finds them. Menu item values are LOWERCASE ('electron', 'proton', 'neutron') — must use lowercase target text or matching fails.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: find_and_click target=<combobox value substring> role=AXComboBox match_mode=contains -> wait 1s -> find_and_click target=<lowercase-answer> role=AXMenuItem match_mode=exact

subtypes.name: free_response
subtypes.description: Open text field for free-form written answers. Perseus widget: free-response. Relatively rare.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click text area -> type response -> click 'Check'

subtypes.name: table_input
subtypes.description: Table with editable cells for filling in values. Perseus widgets: table, matrix.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click table cells -> type values -> click 'Check'

operational_notes.note: CRITICAL GOTCHA: All cell text fields share IDENTICAL name='Your answer:' AND description='Your answer:' AND initial value=''. Comboboxes likewise share name='Select an answer'. find_element only checks Title/Description/Value attributes — so find_and_type ALWAYS returns the FIRST AXTextField in tree order. Two consecutive find_and_type calls overwrite the same first cell. Tested: click element=$tf.element via for_each does NOT make find_and_type respect focus.
operational_notes.disambiguator: find_all role=AXTextField returns each item with a 'label' field (preceding AXStaticText computed by _find_preceding_label). For chemistry table_input: input 1 (Row 1 col atomic-num) label='strontium', input 2 (Row 2 col atomic-num) label='Zn'. Inputs 3-4 are wrapper duplicates with label='Atomic number' (column header) — skip via lookup miss in lookup_match.
operational_notes.handler_required: find_and_type must accept 'element' parameter (parallel to click handler). The working pattern: find_all role=AXTextField -> $textfields, for_each $tf in $textfields: lookup_match {row_label: value} key=$tf.label -> $answer, conditional $answer: find_and_type element=$tf.element text=$answer. Same shape works for the comboboxes after an analogous 'element' parameter is added — though comboboxes have label collisions (both row 1 and row 2 may resolve to same row label depending on tree-walk timing); prefer position-aware disambiguation or sequential 'Select an answer' contains-match relying on Wonder Blocks updating combobox accessible name post-fill.
operational_notes.prior_workaround: None reliable before find_and_type accepted element. Sequential find_and_click 'Select an answer' AXComboBox contains may work IF Wonder Blocks updates the combobox name to the selected value after fill (untested). Even then, text fields cannot be disambiguated by name alone. Best achievable outcome was partial fill (1-2 cells correct) with Check disabled. Do NOT click Skip.

operational_notes.superseded: 2026-05-08T01:46:08Z entry — keyboard-nav pattern was claimed verified once but did not reproduce in production. Mac BT logs confirmed press_key fired (handler executed) but the AX tree showed zero state change after Down + Enter. Root cause traced via Perplexity Deep Research + ChatGPT analysis: Wonder Blocks' onKeyDown handler lives on a <View> wrapper element. When the dropdown opens via mouse_click, document.activeElement is the opener button (not the wrapper); options are React-portaled OUTSIDE the wrapper. So Down/Enter dispatched to the document hit the opener (which closes the menu on ArrowDown), and synthetic clicks on AXMenuItem fire in DOM but don't bubble through React's delegation chain because the portal's root container is document.body.
operational_notes.note: Wonder Blocks listbox-style dropdowns (Khan SingleSelect / DropdownCore — used in table_input chemical-symbol and element-name cells, label_image, and matching exercises) require the VoiceOver path: AXUIElementSetAttributeValue(menu_item, kAXFocusedAttribute, True) drives REAL DOM focus onto the OptionItem, after which AXPress (or kVK_Return) selects through React's normal event chain. This is wrapped in the semantic select_dropdown_option handler (Mac side), which owns the strategy ladder (focus_press → focus_space → focus_enter → mouse_click → ax_press) and verifies the trigger AXValue actually changed before returning success. Spark BTs MUST use select_dropdown_option for combobox/popup widgets — never compose raw click + press_key.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: find_all role=AXComboBox -> $popups, send_to_llm question_type=solve_matching items=$popups -> $matches, for_each $popup in $popups: lookup_match matches=$matches.matches key=$popup.description -> $chosen, select_dropdown_option trigger_element=$popup.element option=$chosen open_strategy=mouse_click open_wait=0.7 verify_wait=0.4. Then find_and_click target='Check' role=AXButton strategy=mouse_click post_delay=2.5.
operational_notes.disambiguator: Wonder Blocks does NOT update combobox accessible name/title/description after selection — only the value attribute changes. select_dropdown_option handles this internally by checking AXValue (not just name) for verification. When passing $popup.element from for_each, each iteration targets the specific combobox by element ref, eliminating the name-collision problem entirely.
operational_notes.handler_required: Mac-side select_dropdown_option semantic handler (added 2026-05-08). Owns: app activation before raw events, full-app AX tree walk (NOT scoped to AXWebArea), normalize 'not selected'/'selected' suffixes, strategy ladder with focus_press as primary, AXValue verification before returning success.

operational_notes.note: Partial-submission UX (verified 2026-05-08): Clicking Check on a table_input with one or more empty cells does NOT mark the question wrong. Khan shows a soft modal in lower-right: 'You're almost there. There are still more parts of this question to answer. Give it another try!' with a 'Try again' AXButton (and 'Skip' still visible). The fills are PRESERVED — clicking 'Try again' dismisses the modal without resetting any cell values. Useful when the BT has filled some cells but not all (e.g. dropdowns done via keyboard nav but text fields blocked by a prior disambiguation gap). To dismiss: find_and_click target='Try again' role=AXButton match_mode=exact -> wait 1.5s. Form returns to fillable state with Check button visible.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: If Check fires with empty cells: find_and_click target='Try again' role=AXButton match_mode=exact -> wait 1.5s -> resume fill. Recoverable, not destructive.

subtypes.name: passage_based
subtypes.description: Reading comprehension with a passage displayed alongside questions. The passage may be long and require scrolling. Perseus widgets: passage, passage-ref. Questions reference specific parts of the passage.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: read passage (may need scroll) -> answer questions (usually MC) -> click 'Check'

submit_button.notes: Located at bottom-right of exercise area. After clicking, the system grades the answer and shows feedback (green/ding for correct, red for incorrect). A brief animation plays. After feedback, a 'Next question' button may appear OR the next question auto-loads. In quizzes/unit tests, the button may show as 'Next' instead of 'Check' after answering. The 'Skip' button appears to the LEFT of 'Check' — do NOT confuse them. Check is the rightmost primary-colored button.

images.purpose: Capture the full exercise question including any diagrams, graphs, images, mathematical notation, or interactive elements that contain visual information needed to answer the question

EXERCISE.wrong_answer_behavior: Incorrect answer: selected answer highlights red, audible tone may play, correct answer may or may not be shown depending on exercise type. For practice exercises: advances to next question (wrong answers reduce score percentage). For quizzes/unit tests: advances to next question, wrong answer recorded, may level down skills. After last question in any set, summary card appears showing skills leveled up/down with option to review. If exactly 1 wrong in practice exercise, 'Bonus question' may be offered.

operational_notes.rule: SMART RETRY WITH PRIOR-ANSWER EXCLUSION: Try again → re-extract → send to LLM with PREVIOUS wrong answer explicitly forbidden → click new answer → wait_for_element Check → submit.
operational_notes.policy_clarification_from_jesse_2026_05_: The auto-retry ban applies to RE-SUBMITTING THE SAME WRONG ANSWER over and over (that triggers bot detection). Claude-mediated retry with a FRESH re-evaluation of the question, producing a different answer, is allowed. Treat the failure as new information that should change the choice.
operational_notes.note: Observed 18:57-18:58: LLM kept picking the SAME wrong combination (Choice A + Choice B with visually-similar arrow diagrams) on every retry because the retry context only said 'previous was wrong' without naming what was previously picked. Without a concrete exclusion, the LLM re-arrives at the same visual conclusion. THE FIX: the worker must extract the prior answer from the failed_bt context in the prompt (metadata.context.user_guidance contains the prior BT JSON and Mac log showing what was clicked), then PRE-FILL the LLM context with 'FORBIDDEN PRIOR ANSWERS: (Choice A) ..., (Choice B) ...'. ALSO: after Try again, Khan should reset checkboxes to unchecked. If state persists (worth verifying empirically), clicking a still-checked box will UNCHECK it — submit lands with zero selected → wrong again.
operational_notes.wrong_answer_behavior: MAX 1 smart retry per question. If the smart-retry fails (same wrong answer or different but still wrong), system lands back on WRONG_ANSWER with diagnosing.flag already set — escalation cycle kicks in normally.

failure_modes_observed.18:58_observation: Worker emitted smart-retry BT with extract_question parent_contains:'Choose' filter. Failed: 'Could not extract question text from tree'. Filter too restrictive.
failure_modes_observed.18:58_fix: extract_question must use {role: AXStaticText, min_length: 20} — no parent_contains.
failure_modes_observed.19:05_observation: Even with smart-retry-rule in place, LLM re-picks same A+B combo because retry context lacks explicit prior-answer exclusion.
failure_modes_observed.19:05_fix: BT generator must include prior $llm.selected in retry context as explicit 'do not pick these' list. Operational_note + worker prompt rule.

operational_notes.screen_variant: ANY checkbox/radio exercise with visual diagrams
operational_notes.rule: CHECK BUTTON TIMING: wait_for_element Check before clicking it after answer selection
operational_notes.note: Khan React UI takes 1-2 seconds to register checkbox state changes and make the Check button responsive. With post_delay: 1.0 after each checkbox click, the find_and_click(Check) immediately after for_each may return None because Check is still in transitional/disabled state. Always insert `wait_for_element role=AXButton target='Check' max_wait=3.0` between the answer-selection for_each and the Check click. Mac btlog evidence 18:57:22: `find_and_click: looking for 'Check' role=AXButton → ACTION RETURNED NONE` exactly 1 second after the last checkbox click.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
Insert AFTER for_each and BEFORE find_and_click(Check):
{"type":"action","action":"wait_for_element","params":{"role":"AXButton","target":"Check","max_wait":3.0}}

operational_notes.screen_variant: ANY radio/checkbox/text exercise that uses extract_question
operational_notes.rule: OPTIONS FROM extract_question: pass options=$q.options to send_to_llm — never hardcode
operational_notes.note: Khan AXCheckBox/AXRadioButton names include the '(Choice X) ' prefix (e.g., '(Choice A) Speed includes direction, but velocity doesn't.'). When the BT hardcodes options in send_to_llm.params as the visible option TEXT only (without the prefix), the LLM returns prefix-less text, and find_and_click with match_mode=exact CANNOT find the AXCheckBox because the actual AX name has the prefix. RESULT: ACTION RETURNED NONE. FIX: always use $q.options from extract_question — those are the raw AX names including the (Choice X) prefix. Then LLM returns the full AX name, find_and_click matches exactly. Observed 20:15:29 — BT hardcoded 4 plain-text option strings, LLM picked 'Speed and velocity both tell you how fast an object is moving.', find_and_click failed (actual AX name was '(Choice D) Speed and velocity both tell you how fast an object is moving.').
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
Use:
{"action":"extract_question","params":{"question":{"role":"AXStaticText","min_length":20},"options":{"role":"AXCheckBox","exclude_titles":["Draw on exercise"]}},"store":"q"},
{"action":"send_to_llm","params":{"question":"$q.question_text","question_type":"solve_checkbox","options":"$q.options","context":"<screen context>"},"store":"llm"},
NEVER write `options: ["plain text option 1", "plain text option 2", ...]` literally.

VIDEO.description: Video screens show an embedded YouTube player (primary) or fallback player in the main content area. Below the video: transcript expandable section, 'Ask a question' discussion box, and community comments. Left sidebar shows lesson position. Video title appears above player. Energy points counter (typically 750-850) updates as video progresses. A correction text box may appear in lower-right of video for known errors (not visible in fullscreen). Some videos have associated questions that pop up during playback.
VIDEO.player_type: embedded YouTube (primary), custom fallback player (low bandwidth/restricted networks)

UNSTARTED.tree_signal: YouTube iframe present but video not playing; large play button triangle overlay visible in center of player; AXGroup containing embedded AXWebArea (YouTube) detected; no progress bar advancement
UNSTARTED.action: Click the play button (large triangle overlay in center of YouTube player) or click anywhere on the video area to start playback. If fallback player is shown instead, look for a play button within the custom player controls.

PLAYING.tree_signal: Video actively playing; YouTube progress bar advancing; energy points incrementing in real-time; no play button overlay visible; elapsed time counter increasing
PLAYING.action: video_poll ONLY — monitor progress. Do NOT interact with the page. Let the video play to completion. Optionally set speed to 2x via gear icon -> Playback speed -> 2x (this is safe and still counts for completion). Do not scrub/skip forward.

COMPLETE.tree_signal: Video ended; replay circular arrow button may appear in player center; full colored progress bar visible below video icon in sidebar; checkmark icon appears on video item in sidebar; energy points fully awarded (750-850); YouTube end screen may show suggested videos (ignore these)
COMPLETE.action: Use the completion_action prescription (visual-first click_at by tree-read bbox). Look at the screenshot, identify the 'Up next: <title>' AXLink in the lower-right of the video player, read its visible_bbox from the AX tree, output click_at at the bbox center. DO NOT use find_and_click with match_mode=contains — Jesse's rule (2026-05-14) is exact-match only. DO NOT click 'Next in course' / 'Previous in course' (tiny top-of-page arrows; course-wide nav). DO NOT click YouTube suggested videos or 'Video on YouTube' link.

never_click_extras.reason: Course-wide navigation arrow (~23x25px) at top of page. Skips PAST the current unit entirely. The right post-video target is the 'Up next: <title>' AXLink, NOT this arrow.
Course-wide navigation arrow. Skips BACKWARD past completed content. Never the right target for advancement.

completion_action.rationale: The 'Up next:' link text varies per video ('Up next: introduction', 'Up next: quadratics', etc.). Per Jesse's rule (2026-05-14): NO match_mode=contains anywhere. For variable-text targets, use click_at with bbox coordinates read from the AX tree at decision time.
process: 1. Look at the screenshot. The post-video advancement target is the 'Up next: <title>' link, prominently rendered in the lower-right of the video player area (approximately 135x40 px, AXLink role).
2. Find that element in the AX tree (it will have role=AXLink and its name will start with 'Up next:').
3. Read its visible_bbox from the tree node: visible_bbox = [x, y, width, height].
4. Compute the click target: cx = x + width/2, cy = y + height/2.
5. Output a BT with action=click_at and params={x: cx, y: cy}. No find_and_click. No contains.
completion_action.fallback_if_no_up_next_link: Find the next unchecked item in the left sidebar (sidebar items are AXLink with role=AXLink, contained inside the sidebar AXOutline or AXList region). Read its visible_bbox. Output click_at with bbox center.
completion_action.anti_pattern: NEVER use find_and_click with match_mode=contains. NEVER click the small (~23x25 px) 'Next in course' or 'Previous in course' AXButton arrows at the top of the page — those are course-wide navigation chrome, not lesson advancement.

images.purpose: Describe the video content frame if transcript is unavailable or for visual-heavy content

VIDEO.speed_controls: YouTube player offers 0.25x, 0.5x, 0.75x, 1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, 1.75x, 2x speeds via the gear/settings icon in the YouTube control bar. Speeds up to 2x do NOT affect completion credit. The fallback player may lack speed controls entirely. To change speed: click gear icon in YouTube player controls -> 'Playback speed' -> select desired speed. 2x recommended for time efficiency.

operational_notes.rule: Post-video advancement uses 'Up next' link, NEVER 'Replay Video'
operational_notes.note: When a Khan video completes, the screen shows two affordances near the player: a 'Replay Video' button (do NOT click — re-watching wastes compute and never advances mastery) and an 'Up next: <content_type>' link (where content_type is 'video', 'article', 'exercise', etc). The Up next link is the ONLY correct advancement target. Its FULL text varies by next content type, so use description-substring filtering for find_all, NOT match_mode: contains on the click step.
anti_patterns: click_at on hardcoded coordinates (positions shift with viewport)
find_and_click target='Up next' match_mode=contains (BANNED — match_mode must be exact)
click Replay Video (wastes compute, doesn't advance)
click 'Next in course' (advances LESSONS not VIDEOS — overshoots)

ARTICLE.description: Article screens present formatted educational text in the main content area with headers, paragraphs, images, embedded interactive elements ('Try it yourself' inline practice problems rendered by Perseus), and mathematical notation (MathJax/KaTeX). Left sidebar shows lesson position. 'Up next' link or arrow appears at bottom to advance. Articles may contain embedded Perseus widgets for inline practice that are NOT separately graded. Breadcrumb navigation at top shows Course > Unit > Lesson path.
ARTICLE.completion_mechanism: Articles auto-complete when visited and scrolled through. No explicit 'Mark as complete' button. Opening and scrolling to bottom registers as read. For teacher assignments, viewing the article marks it complete with a green checkmark. The 'Up next' navigation advances to next activity.

completion_button.notes: Not a completion button per se — articles auto-complete on view/scroll. 'Up next' is a navigation link at the bottom of the article or visible in the sidebar that advances to the next activity in the lesson. May also appear as a right-arrow icon or as the next item's title as a clickable link in the sidebar.

images.purpose: Capture article content including diagrams, charts, illustrations, or embedded interactive elements

NAVIGATION.description: Navigation screens include Course pages (list of Units with mastery %), Unit pages (list of Lessons with completion indicators), and Lesson list pages (list of Videos, Articles, Exercises in chronological order). Course pages have 'Course challenge' card at bottom. Unit pages have quiz/test links. Left sidebar shows assignments (ordered by due date) at top and lesson content below. Items have type icons: camera for video, document for article, star/diamond for exercise. Navigation is also possible via breadcrumbs at top of page.

done: Full colored progress bar under item

NAVIGATION.navigation_model: sidebar + list. Left sidebar shows lesson-level navigation in chronological order (videos, articles, exercises listed sequentially). Course and Unit pages use vertical list/card layout. Breadcrumbs at top: Course > Unit > Lesson. Items within a lesson listed vertically with type icons (camera=video, document=article, star=exercise). Sidebar has collapsible arrow and shows assignments section at top ordered by due date.
NAVIGATION.adaptive_routing_warning: Khan Academy has 'Up next' recommendation system that suggests next activity based on learner's progress and mastery level. This is generally safe to follow for sequential completion. However, the Mastery system may recommend skipping ahead or reviewing earlier content based on quiz/test performance. Mastery Challenges appear only when conditions are met (3+ Familiar, 1+ Proficient skills). For complete coursework automation, follow the sidebar lesson list sequentially rather than relying solely on 'Up next' recommendations. The 'recommended' path may skip optional content or suggest Mastery Challenges instead of sequential lesson items.

operational_notes.rule: NAV CLICK BY ELEMENT REF: don't rely on find_and_click + exact text match for nav links
operational_notes.note: Khan sidebar nav AXLink names can include status suffixes (e.g., 'Apply: ... Not started') that don't concatenate cleanly into the AX name field — the suffix may be a separate child text node. find_and_click with match_mode=exact then fails because the LLM's chosen label doesn't match the AXLink's actual name attribute. Observed 20:55:17: LLM picked 'Apply: Connecting motion and forces Not started', find_and_click returned None. FIX: use the same pattern that worked for dropdowns — pass the raw element ref from find_all instead of name-matching. send_to_llm with navigate gets items=$links; the LLM returns answer=<chosen_description>; instead of find_and_click target=$nav.answer, do match_to_option server-side to find the matching item index, then click $links[index].element directly.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: Until server-side parse_navigate returns matched_index, the workaround is: for_each over $links, conditional check if $link.description == $nav.answer, click element=$link.element on match. OR use match_mode=contains as a soft fallback (but contains is BANNED per Jesse — exact-only). Better: enhance parse_navigate server-side to return both answer (text) and selected (element ref dict).

TRANSITION.description: Transition screens appear between activities and at exercise boundaries. Types: (1) Exercise summary cards showing skills leveled up/down with colored indicators after completing exercise/quiz/test, (2) Welcome modals ('So we can give you the right tools, let us know if you're a... Learner/Teacher/Parent'), (3) District admin goal-setting popups on login, (4) 'Keep learning' prompts, (5) Mastery level-up celebrations with confetti/animations, (6) Cookie consent banners, (7) Quiz/test intro cards showing question count and estimated time, (8) Khanmigo feature announcements.

operational_notes.rule: POST-COMPLETION ADVANCE: click 'Up next: <type>' AXLink — that IS the right click here
operational_notes.note: After a correct exercise, after Show summary, after a video reaches end: the screen shows an 'Up next: <type>' affordance (AXLink). On these completion/transition screens, that link is the canonical advance — equivalent to picking the next sidebar item but cleaner because the AXLink name is precise. The mastery-adaptive-skip risk from the old broad 'never click Up next' rule does NOT apply here — that risk is for mid-content Up next, not post-completion.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: Use find_all role=AXLink description_contains='Up next' -> $up_next_links; for_each $link in $up_next_links: click element=$link.element strategy=mouse_click post_delay=3.5. Only 1-2 such links typically present; safe to click all (the second is rare and they all advance to the same destination).
anti_patterns: find_and_click target='Up next: exercise' role=AXLink match_mode=exact — fragile because exact text varies by next-content-type
Sidebar navigation when Up next is available — slower, more LLM call cost

UNKNOWN.description: Fallback for unrecognized screens. Could be: account settings, teacher dashboard (wrong account type), Khanmigo AI chat interface, donation/fundraising page, search results, community discussion forums, user profile/badges page, parent/coach dashboard, or any non-learning page.

accessibility_tree_guide.focus_area: AXWebArea subtree (skip AXMenuBar, AXToolbar, AXTabGroup). Focus on the main content area which contains the exercise/video/article rendered by Perseus/React. The left sidebar navigation is an AXGroup or AXNavigation role containing AXLink elements for lesson items. YouTube player is a nested AXWebArea inside the main AXWebArea.

key_signals.AXRadioButton: Multiple choice answer option in Perseus exercise. Each radio button = one answer choice. Label text is the answer content. Parent group is the answer choice set.
key_signals.AXCheckBox: Multi-select answer option ('Select all that apply'). Each checkbox = one possible answer.
key_signals.AXTextField: Numeric or expression input field in Perseus. Also appears in search bars (ignore). Exercise inputs are within perseus-renderer or framework-perseus containers.
key_signals.AXTextArea: Free-response text input. Rare in exercises. Also used for 'Ask a question' discussion box below videos (ignore discussion areas).
key_signals.AXButton: Interactive buttons. Key: 'Check' (submit), 'Skip' (skip, marks incorrect), 'Next question' (advance), 'Bonus question' (post-exercise). Also: sidebar collapse arrow, video player controls (fallback player).
key_signals.AXLink: Navigation links. Sidebar lesson items, 'Up next' advancement, breadcrumbs, course/unit/lesson links. DANGER: 'Video on YouTube' and 'Report a problem' are also AXLink — never click these.
key_signals.AXPopUpButton: Dropdown select widget in Perseus exercises. Click to open, then select from popup menu items.
key_signals.AXGroup: Container for grouped content. Video player = AXGroup containing YouTube iframe AXWebArea. Graph widgets = AXGroup. Question blocks = AXGroup within Perseus renderer.
key_signals.AXImage: Images within exercises, articles, or as icons. Exercise images may contain visual info needed to answer questions. Type icons in sidebar navigation.
key_signals.AXStaticText: Text content. Question text, answer choice labels, article paragraphs, video titles. Primary source for extracting question content for LLM processing.
key_signals.AXProgressIndicator: Progress bars. Course/unit mastery %, video playback progress, exercise question progress (e.g., '3 of 7').
key_signals.AXList: Ordered/unordered list. Appears in sorter/orderer Perseus widgets where items need rearranging. Also in article content.
key_signals.AXHeading: Section headers in articles and navigation pages. Useful for identifying content type and page structure.

completion_indicators_in_tree.video_complete: AXImage with checkmark near video item in sidebar navigation. Progress bar (AXProgressIndicator) at 100%. Full colored bar visible.
completion_indicators_in_tree.exercise_complete: AXStaticText showing score (e.g., '100%' or '5 of 7 correct') on summary card. Sidebar item shows checkmark AXImage.
completion_indicators_in_tree.article_complete: AXImage with green checkmark near article item in sidebar. Auto-completes on visit/scroll.
completion_indicators_in_tree.skill_mastered: AXStaticText 'Mastered' or crown AXImage near skill name in navigation/progress views.
completion_indicators_in_tree.skill_proficient: AXStaticText 'Proficient' near skill name in navigation/progress views.
completion_indicators_in_tree.question_correct: Green-highlighted answer area. AXStaticText 'Correct!' after Check. Audible ding sound.
completion_indicators_in_tree.question_incorrect: Red-highlighted answer area. AXStaticText 'Incorrect' or 'Sorry, try again' or 'Not quite' after Check.

platform_specific_roles.meaning: Perseus widget container. Look inside for AXRadioButton, AXTextField, AXPopUpButton, AXList etc. to determine specific widget type and required interaction pattern.

platform_specific_roles.context: Nested within main AXWebArea, inside video content area
platform_specific_roles.meaning: Embedded YouTube player iframe. Interaction with YouTube controls (play, pause, settings gear, progress bar) happens within this nested web area. Avoid clicking YouTube suggested videos at end.
Lesson sidebar containing AXLink elements for all activities in the current lesson. Items listed chronologically. Collapsible via arrow button. Shows assignments at top.
Exercise control buttons. 'Check' = rightmost, primary-colored (blue/green). 'Skip' = to its left, secondary-colored. After answering, 'Check' may change to 'Next question' or 'Next'.

platform_specific_roles.context: Bottom of article/video page or in sidebar
platform_specific_roles.meaning: 'Up next' navigation link to advance to next activity in lesson sequence. May also be the next item's title rendered as a link.
Multiple choice answer option. The AXRadioButton's AXDescription or AXValue contains the answer text. Click to select, then click 'Check' to submit.

button_reference.behavior: Submits currently selected/entered answer for grading. Shows correct (green + ding) or incorrect (red) feedback. Advances question flow.
Skips current question and marks it as INCORRECT. Shows step-by-step solution. Cannot be undone. Only use if genuinely stuck.
Advances to next question in exercise set after current answer checked.

button_reference.location: Bottom-right of quiz/test question or bottom of content
button_reference.behavior: Advances to next question in quiz/unit test/course challenge, or to next content item.
Appears after exercise with exactly 1 wrong answer. Presents additional question; correct = upgrades to Proficient. MUST be answered or skipped if offered.

button_reference.location: Bottom of content area or in sidebar navigation
button_reference.behavior: Navigates to next activity in lesson sequence (video/article/exercise). Primary inter-activity navigation.
Begins assessment. Info card shows question count and estimated time first.
Begins a quiz assessment.
Begins a unit test assessment.
Begins the 30-question course challenge.
Dismisses interstitial screens and continues to next content or resumes progress.
Returns to lesson flow after viewing exercise results summary.
Selects 'Learner' role in onboarding modal. Sets UI for student experience. Click to dismiss modal.
Dismisses informational tooltips, feature announcements, cookie banners.
Accepts cookies and dismisses consent banner.

button_reference.location: Modal dialogs, Khanmigo chat overlay, video modals
button_reference.behavior: Closes modal windows including Khanmigo overlay, info popups, video modals opened from navigation pages.
Dismisses optional feature prompts without enabling them.
Shows hints for current question. In current UI this is integrated into Skip flow. Using may affect scoring perception.
Opens content issue reporting dialog. Does NOT advance learning. Contains hidden 'Show Answer'. May flag account.

button_reference.location: Bottom of video page below comments
button_reference.behavior: Navigates away to YouTube.com. No progress tracked, no credit awarded, no energy points, assignments not marked complete.
Reloads video in YouTube player from fallback player. Causes page reload. Only relevant if fallback player was served.
Ends session. Unsaved quiz/test progress may be lost. Requires re-authentication.
Alternative text for logout. Same effect as Log out.
Navigates to donation page. Not related to coursework.
Opens search overlay. Could navigate away from current lesson flow if result clicked.
Opens course catalog. Could navigate away from current lesson.
Selects 'Teacher' role — wrong role for student coursework automation. Sets UI for teacher dashboard.
Selects 'Parent' role — wrong role for student coursework automation. Sets UI for parent/coach view.
